description: Fix duplicate terminal output in Execute phase by removing direct stderr writes and routing output through the existing callback infrastructure

userStories.title: Remove direct stderr writes from executor
userStories.description: As a CLI user running issue-flow in a terminal, I want the Execute phase to show iteration progress only inside the listr2 task tree, so that I see a clean, non-duplicated terminal UI.
acceptanceCriteria: The process.stderr.write() call in executor.ts (lines 25-28) is removed
The JSDoc comment on lines 9-10 is updated to reflect that output is no longer forwarded to stderr
Running the Execute phase in a TTY terminal shows iteration output only within the listr2 tree — no text appears below it

userStories.title: Route executor output through the output callback
userStories.description: As a developer debugging with --verbose, I want Claude's raw output to be routed through the global output callback, so that I can still see it in verbose mode without breaking the single-writer pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: When an output callback is set (listr2 active), Claude's trimmed output is forwarded through it
When no callback is set (e.g., tests, standalone usage), the output is silently discarded (it is already returned via ClaudeResult)
Verbose mode (--verbose) shows Claude output within the listr2 renderer output area

userStories.title: Verify non-TTY / CI output remains readable
userStories.description: As a CI pipeline consumer, I want the Execute phase to produce readable sequential output under the listr2 simple renderer, so that CI logs remain useful after this fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Running with TERM=dumb or piped stdout produces legible, non-duplicated output
No information is lost compared to the current simple-renderer behavior (the output callback still fires under the simple renderer)
userStories.notes: Verified: setOutputCallback fires in all renderer paths (default/verbose/simple). No direct stderr writes in executor.ts. CI/non-TTY output is legible and non-duplicated.
